Title: A new cyanogenic metabolite in Arabidopsis required for inducible pathogen defense

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen elicitation including Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato avrRpm1 (Psta), flg22 peptide, and biotic stress conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inducible pathogen defense; contributes to resistance against bacterial and fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling tissue and leaves after pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: New branch of indole metabolism distinct from the canonical camalexin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for 4-OH-ICN formation; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Insertion mutant analysis; untargeted metabolomics; LC-MS metabolite profiling; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro enzyme assay; NMR structural confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for accumulation of 4-OH-ICN; loss increases susceptibility to Pseudomonas syringae and Botrytis cinerea
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced under biotic stress and pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen-inducible in Arabidopsis seedlings and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics datasets from NASCArrays biotic stress experiments; untargeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering inducible disease-resistance metabolites or reconstructing the 4-OH-ICN pathway in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82C2 was identified from pathogen-induced Arabidopsis P450s by transcriptome mining and metabolomics. 2. It catalyzes 4-hydroxylation of ICN to produce 4-OH-ICN. 3. cyp82C2 mutants are deficient in 4-OH-ICN pathway metabolites and show increased pathogen susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 4-OH-ICN is unstable and can hydrolyze to 4-OH-ICA during assays; CYP82C2 acts on ICN rather than ICA.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen elicitation including Psta and fungal infection
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early shared branch of indole-derived defense metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidation step leading from indole-3-acetaldoxime-derived intermediates toward ICN biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor pool for ICN/4-OH-ICN branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Insertion mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; heterologous pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for accumulation of ICN and downstream 4-OH-ICN metabolites; mutants show altered disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway engineering component for reconstructing ICN-derived defense metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A12 participates in the newly identified ICN/4-OH-ICN defense metabolite pathway. 2. Mutants have reduced pathway metabolites, supporting an essential biosynthetic role. 3. Transient expression contributes to pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP71A12 is also connected to other indolic defense metabolism branches, so effects may reflect shared precursor routing.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts the aryl cyanohydrin intermediate to ICN in the ICN pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for ICN and downstream 4-OH-ICN accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important component for metabolic engineering of cyanogenic indole defense compounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FOX1 is part of the complete biosynthetic pathway to 4-OH-ICN. 2. It functions downstream of cyanohydrin formation to generate ICN. 3. Mutant and heterologous expression data support its necessity for pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named in the paper as a pathway enzyme uncovered by co-expression and validated by reconstitution.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen elicitation including Psta and fungal infection
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Aryl cyanohydrin intermediate leading to ICN
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts IAOx toward an aryl cyanohydrin intermediate used for ICN biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to ICN and supplies flux to 4-OH-ICN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; heterologous expression and pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for full production of ICN pathway metabolites and contributes to disease resistance phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shared role with camalexin biosynthesis may complicate engineering specificity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with downstream enzymes to reconstruct the new defense pathway, but shared functions with camalexin branch should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A13 contributes an upstream step in the 4-OH-ICN pathway by generating a cyanohydrin intermediate from IAOx. 2. Mutants show reduced pathway metabolites. 3. Along with CYP71A12 and FOX1, it enables pathway reconstruction in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This enzyme also functions in camalexin-related metabolism, indicating pathway branching from shared indolic precursors.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen elicitation in Arabidopsis; transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis elicited tissue; reconstituted in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tryptophan-derived indole metabolism feeding the 4-OH-ICN pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step from tryptophan to indole-3-acetaldoxime
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tryptophan to indole-3-acetaldoxime, supplying precursor for ICN and related indolic defense pathways
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides precursor necessary to reconstruct ICN and 4-OH-ICN pathway metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Feeds multiple defense branches, so manipulation could broadly alter indolic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream precursor-supply enzyme for pathway reconstruction in synthetic biology
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B2 supplies IAOx, the common precursor for the newly identified 4-OH-ICN pathway. 2. Its expression with downstream enzymes reconstructs pathway metabolites in N. benthamiana. 3. It functions as the pathway entry point rather than a unique terminal determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included by the authors in transient pathway reconstruction; highly upstream and shared with other indolic pathways.
